name: Multi Leg Order Book
summary: Retrieves the Multi Leg Order Book via a POST call. On success returns a JSON array of order objects (covering single, two-leg / 2L and three-leg / 3L orders) with per-leg trading symbols, quantities, prices, traded quantities and average trade prices; on failure returns a JSON object with stat, request_time and emsg.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Order book success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: exch
response_fields.description: Exchange Segment.

response_fields.name: tsym
response_fields.description: Trading symbol / contract on which order is placed.

response_fields.name: norenordno
response_fields.description: Noren Order Number.

response_fields.name: prc*
response_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero".

response_fields.name: qty*
response_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: prd
response_fields.description: Display product alias name, using prarr returned in user details.

response_fields.name: status
response_fields.description: Order status.

response_fields.name: trantype*
response_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

response_fields.name: prctyp
response_fields.description: Price type.

response_fields.name: fillshares
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of this order.

response_fields.name: avgprc
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity.

response_fields.name: rejreason
response_fields.description: If order is rejected, reason in text form.

response_fields.name: exchordid
response_fields.description: Exchange Order Number.

response_fields.name: cancelqty
response_fields.description: Canceled quantity for order which is in status cancelled.

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: dscqty*
response_fields.description: Order disclosed quantity [If dscqty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: trgprc
response_fields.description: Order trigger price.

response_fields.name: ret*
response_fields.description: Order validity [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject].

response_fields.name: uid
response_fields.description: (Blank description in the source.)

response_fields.name: actid
response_fields.description: (Blank description in the source.)

response_fields.name: bpprc
response_fields.description: Book Profit Price applicable only if product is selected as B (Bracket order).

response_fields.name: blprc
response_fields.description: Book loss Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: trailprc
response_fields.description: Trailing Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: amo*
response_fields.description: The message "Invalid AMO" will be displayed if the "amo" field is not sent with a "Yes" value. If amo is not required, do not send this field.

response_fields.name: pp
response_fields.description: Price precision.

response_fields.name: ti
response_fields.description: Tick size.

response_fields.name: ls
response_fields.description: Lot size.

response_fields.name: tsym2
response_fields.description: Trading symbol of second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

response_fields.name: trantype2
response_fields.description: Transaction type of second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

response_fields.name: qty2
response_fields.description: Quantity for second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

response_fields.name: prc2
response_fields.description: Price for second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

response_fields.name: tsym3
response_fields.description: Trading symbol of third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

response_fields.name: trantype3
response_fields.description: Transaction type of third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

response_fields.name: qty3
response_fields.description: Quantity for third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

response_fields.name: prc3
response_fields.description: Price for third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

response_fields.name: fillshares2
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of 2nd Leg.

response_fields.name: avgprc2
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity for 2nd leg.

response_fields.name: fillshares3
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of 3rd Leg.

response_fields.name: avgprc3
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity for 3rd leg.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: (Failure case) Order book failure indication.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: (Failure case) Response received time.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: (Failure case) Error message.

sample_success_response: Response data will be in json Array of objects with below fields in case of success. (No verbatim JSON example block is shown in the document; the success response is a JSON array of objects with the success fields listed above, e.g. stat = "Ok".)
sample_failure_response:
Response data will be in json format with below fields in case of failure:
stat = Not_Ok (Order book failure indication.)
request_time (Response received time.)
emsg (Error message.)
(No verbatim JSON example block is provided in the document for the failure case.)
notes:
- The base URL in the curl example is a placeholder ('https://BaseURL/MultiLegOrderBook'); the real endpoint is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/MultiLegOrderBook.
- The request is sent as a form-style POST: jData is a JSON object and jKey is appended as a separate parameter (jData={...}&jKey=...).
- The curl --data block in the source contains a stray smart/curly double-quote after the closing brace ( }“&jKey=... ) — an OCR/encoding artifact; a correct payload would close with a normal quote: }'&jKey=... .
- The only documented request body (jData) fields are uid* (required) and prd (optional, H / M / ...).
- In the response/order-book field table, the fields marked with a trailing '*' are: prc*, qty*, trantype*, dscqty*, ret*, amo*.
- amo: must be sent with value "Yes" or the API returns "Invalid AMO"; if AMO is not required, omit the field entirely.
- Multi-leg fields tsym2/trantype2/qty2/prc2 (plus fillshares2/avgprc2) are mandatory for price type 2L and 3L; tsym3/trantype3/qty3/prc3 (plus fillshares3/avgprc3) are mandatory for price type 3L.
- bpprc applies only for product B (Bracket); blprc and trailprc apply for products H (High Leverage) and B (Bracket).
- prd possible values are shown as 'H / M / ...' (truncated list in the doc).
- The response fields uid and actid appear in the source table with blank possible-value and blank description cells.
- uid is documented both as a request jData body field and as a response field.
issues:
- No verbatim JSON success/failure example blocks are present in the document for this endpoint; only field tables and a curl request are shown.
- The curl --data string has an OCR artifact (curly closing quote }“ before &jKey), so the exact intended quoting of the body is slightly ambiguous; reproduced verbatim.
- The response fields uid and actid have blank descriptions and blank possible-value cells in the source.
- uid appears in both the request (jData) field list and the response field list.
